data.content:
Plan for foolery-96ba (Consolidate buildAgentLabel into canonical formatter):

Investigation (already done):
- buildAgentLabel is defined in src/components/agent-history-utils.tsx:403, exported.
- One caller: src/components/agent-history-session-card.tsx:50 (via useMemo, passes session.agentName, session.agentModel, session.agentVersion).
- Canonical formatter lives in src/lib/agent-identity.ts: formatAgentDisplayLabel(AgentIdentityLike) and parseAgentDisplayParts(AgentIdentityLike).
- Tests: no tests assert on the string output of buildAgentLabel. Tests touching agent-history (agent-history-summaries.test.ts, etc.) use a separate agentLabel field on the token-usage summary structure, unrelated to the session card formatter.

Implementation:
1. Update src/components/agent-history-session-card.tsx to import formatAgentDisplayLabel from @/lib/agent-identity and call it directly with { command: session.agentName, model: session.agentModel, version: session.agentVersion } instead of buildAgentLabel.
2. Handle the empty-input case: formatAgentDisplayLabel always returns at least "Unknown", so guard with a check on whether any of the fields are present, returning undefined when none are (preserves current callsite behavior of conditional rendering when agentLabel is undefined).
3. Delete buildAgentLabel from src/components/agent-history-utils.tsx along with the now-unused formatModelDisplay import from @/hooks/use-agent-info.
4. Re-run lint, typecheck, test, build to verify nothing broke.

Output format change: buildAgentLabel produced "Claude · Opus 4.7" with a "·" separator. formatAgentDisplayLabel produces "Claude Opus 4.7" with spaces. Per knot description and contract rule 6, this is acceptable — the canonical formatter is authoritative.

Out of scope (siblings):
- foolery-988b is editing src/lib/agent-identity.ts (OpenCode normaliser). Will not touch.
- foolery-c257 is editing src/components/beat-table-metadata.tsx. Will not touch.

Risk: low. One caller, no test fixtures asserting the dot separator.
